name: OfficeFatigue
alternateName: OfficeFatigue Signal and Benchmark Label Release
description: OfficeFatigue is a wrist-worn multimodal dataset and benchmark for cognitive and physical fatigue sensing in office environments. The current public release combines participant-level processed PPG and 6-axis IMU signal arrays hosted on Hugging Face with per-participant cognitive and physical fatigue label CSV files and benchmark utilities hosted on GitHub.

distribution.name: hf_dataset_card
distribution.description: Hugging Face dataset card for the OfficeFatigue signal release.

distribution.name: hf_signal_files
distribution.description: Participant-level processed PPG and 6-axis IMU signal arrays hosted on Hugging Face.

distribution.name: github_labels
distribution.description: Per-participant OFS and OFL label CSV files hosted in the GitHub repository.

distribution.name: github_code
distribution.description: Benchmark code, components, scripts, configuration files, and documentation hosted in the GitHub repository.

recordSet.name: signal_arrays
recordSet.description: Participant-level processed signal arrays. The expected local layout is pXX/OFS_signal.npy and pXX/OFL_signal.npy. Each array is sampled at 125 Hz and has shape [samples, channels].

field.name: participant_id
field.description: Pseudonymous participant identifier from p01 to p25.

field.name: subset
field.description: Dataset subset: OFS for controlled short-office sessions or OFL for naturalistic long-office routines.

field.name: sampling_rate_hz
field.description: Sampling rate of the PPG and IMU signals.

recordSet.name: label_rows
recordSet.description: Per-participant label rows stored in GitHub CSV files. Each row is aligned to a contiguous 30-second signal segment in benchmark order. The current label CSV files contain only cognitive and physical fatigue labels.

field.name: participant_id
field.description: Pseudonymous participant identifier inferred from the label filename.

field.name: subset
field.description: OFS or OFL, inferred from the label filename.

field.name: row_index
field.description: Zero-based row index. Row order defines alignment to the local signal segment order.

recordSet.name: benchmark_outputs
recordSet.description: Output files written by the benchmark scripts: metrics_by_fold.csv, predictions_by_unit.csv, and summary.csv.

field.name: metrics_by_fold_csv
field.description: Per-fold benchmark metrics under leave-one-participant-out evaluation.

field.name: predictions_by_unit_csv
field.description: Predictions for each evaluated unit or segment.

rai:dataCollection: Data were collected from human participants wearing a wrist-worn device during controlled office sessions and naturalistic office routines, with informed consent.
rai:dataUseCases: The dataset is intended for research on wearable sensing, multimodal time-series learning, office fatigue modeling, and subject-independent evaluation.
rai:dataLimitations: The current Hugging Face release provides participant-level processed signal arrays, while the GitHub repository provides label CSV files and benchmark utilities. Labels are subjective fatigue annotations rather than clinical measurements.
rai:dataBiases: The participant pool, office routines, and sensor placement may not represent all populations, job roles, or workplace environments.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Processed signals are hosted on Hugging Face. Labels, benchmark scripts, utilities, and documentation are maintained in the GitHub repository.
rai:prohibitedUses: Employee surveillance, clinical diagnosis, safety-critical decision making, and attempts to re-identify participants are prohibited.
citation: Anonymous. OfficeFatigue: A Naturalistic Multimodal Dataset for Cognitive and Physical Fatigue. NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks Track, 2026.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The dataset contains human-subject wearable physiological and motion signals, including PPG and 6-axis IMU traces, as well as subjective fatigue and workload self-reports. Participant identifiers are pseudonymous and timestamps are session-relative, but physiological and motion patterns may still carry residual re-identification risk. The release therefore includes responsible-use restrictions and prohibits re-identification attempts.
rai:dataSocialImpact: OfficeFatigue is intended to support research on wearable sensing, multimodal time-series learning, and office fatigue modeling. Potential positive impacts include reproducible benchmarks for human-centered sensing and better understanding of cognitive and physical fatigue during desk work. Potential negative impacts include misuse for workplace surveillance, inappropriate clinical interpretation, or safety-critical decisions; these uses are explicitly prohibited.
